rejection_reason: Semantically strong but veterinary-specific, so it was not as representative for a general human pilot anchor set.
Acceptable candidate, but it depended on linked organization context and lost to the fully self-contained Patient selections.
Strong candidate, but omitted to avoid over-concentrating the six-slot Observation set on overlapping body-measure examples.
Good numeric lab example, but glucose was preferred as the single pilot lab anchor because it is more universally familiar.
Clean numeric Observation, but the device-oriented coding and patient-as-performer pattern made it less general-purpose for a first pilot.
Low complexity, but less ideal as a direct Condition gold anchor because the semantics skew toward family-history context.
Not selected because differential-status semantics and extra asserter linkage make it more context-dependent than the chosen Conditions.
